source_jobs.title: Regional Treasury Operations Specialist - Francophone Markets
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You'll own the end-to-end execution of liquidity workflows and partner operations across our Francophone corridors. Reporting to the Head of Trading & Treasury, this is high-impact individual contributor work that combines analytical rigor with operational ownership. You'll ensure liquidity is optimised, trades are executed accurately, and regional operations run seamlessly in a fast-paced, cross-border environment. You're not just reporting on performance. You're driving it daily, forecasting demand, executing positions, managing partner relationships, and closing the loop on every discrepancy.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Own daily and weekly liquidity forecasting across regional wallets and accounts. Ensure funding levels are optimised to meet transaction demand while adhering to exposure limits.

 - Support and execute regional trading activity in coordination with the Liquidity Manager. Manage currency positions within defined parameters, maintaining clear and proactive communication with the Treasury team.

 - Act as the primary point of contact for regional partners and aggregators. Ensure operational stability while identifying opportunities to improve partner performance and processes.

 - Take full ownership of the reconciliation lifecycle. Track funds across the settlement chain, resolve discrepancies, and ensure accurate reporting to Finance and Group teams.

 - Serve as the regional subject matter expert. Provide insights on settlement trends, partner performance and operational risks that may impact delivery.




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - Proven experience in Treasury Operations, FX, or within fintech and remittance environments. Experience with XOF/XAF regions is a strong advantage.

 - Native-level bilingual proficiency in French and English. Essential.

 - Strong analytical capability with experience in forecasting, liquidity management and data-driven decision making. Comfortable using treasury tools and building or improving models.

 - Solid understanding of cross-border payments, settlement cycles and reconciliation processes.

 - A detail-oriented operator. You take pride in accuracy, close the loop on every discrepancy, and thrive on precision.

 - Ability to communicate clearly and concisely with both internal teams and external partners.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in West African or Central African corridor operations.

 - Familiarity with specific treasury platforms (e.g., Murex, FIS, etc.).

 - Background in payments fintech or remittance companies.



WHY JOIN LEMFI?

Love shouldn’t be expensive, yet those working hardest for their families often face predatory fees and banking exclusion. We're changing this.

At LemFi, you won’t be just a cog in a machine. Whether designing products, scaling operations, or telling our story, you’ll tackle complex challenges with real, immediate impact. Your work goes beyond metrics - it puts money back in families’ pockets and offers access to the previously excluded. Join us to make a meaningful difference, where high performance is a lifeline for millions.

You can connect with us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/lemfi/ and Instagram https://www.instagram.com/uselemfi/ and if you haven't already, download the app on the App Store https://apps.apple.com/gb

source_jobs.title: Senior FinCrime Ops Manager
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You'll own and run LemFi's Financial Crime Operations, sitting at the heart of how we protect customers while keeping the product fast, accessible, and fair. This is hands-on operations leadership. You're accountable for live decisioning, investigations, unblocks, and the continuous improvement of in-life controls. You'll manage a team of 20-30 analysts, and you'll design, run, and improve an operation that balances fraud prevention, customer experience, and growth in a high-volume, fast-moving environment.

You won't just manage queues. You'll think like an operator, move like a business owner, and build a team that makes consistent, risk-based decisions at scale.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Lead the day-to-day Financial Crime Operations function. Own investigations, unblocks and fraud decisioning for in-life customers, and set the standard for how work flows through the operation.

 - Own operational outcomes: time-to-unblock, investigation turnaround, throughput, decision quality, and SLA performance as volumes scale.

 - Continuously assess and improve operational controls to reduce false positives and unnecessary friction. Use real operational insight, not just design-on-paper thinking.

 - Partner with Product and Data to refine rules, unblock logic, and experiments that improve conversion while maintaining risk standards.

 - Manage and mentor a team of 20-30 Financial Crime analysts. Build a culture of ownership, sound judgement, and customer awareness.

 - Monitor trends in alerts, unblocks, false positives and investigation outcomes. Translate data into practical operational changes that improve speed, quality and customer outcomes.

 - Collaborate with Compliance and the MLRO on escalations, SARs and regulatory matters. Act as the operational voice in discussions around risk, controls, and regulatory expectations.




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - 6+ years in Fraud or Financial Crime Operations, with at least 3 years in a leadership role.

 - Strong experience managing live investigations, unblocks, alerts and decisioning in a fintech or high-volume financial environment.

 - Proven ability to run and improve operational workflows, SLAs and performance metrics.

 - Experience partnering closely with Product, Data or Engineering teams on control performance and operational improvements.

 - Solid analytical capability (Excel or similar tools) to assess trends and operational effectiveness.

 - Leadership experience managing and developing teams at scale.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience working with machine learning or rules-based decisioning systems.

 - Cross-border financial operations experience.






WHY JOIN LEMFI?

Love shouldn’t be expensive, yet those working hardest for their families often face predatory fees and banking exclusion. We're changing this.

At LemFi, you won’t be just a cog in a machine. Whether designing products, scaling operations, or telling our story, you’ll tackle complex challenges with real, immediate impact. Your work goes beyond metrics - it puts money back in families’ pockets and offers access to the previously excluded. Join us to make a meaningful difference, where high performance is a lifeline for millions.

You can connect with us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/lemfi/ and In

source_jobs.title: Growth Manager - US to Nigeria
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You'll drive LemFi's expansion across Nigerian communities in the United States. This is a growth role built on travel, community and hands-on execution. You'll spend 3 to 5 days every 2 to 3 weeks in a new US city, representing LemFi at Nigerian community events, signing up new users, arranging sponsorships and networking with Nigerian communities across the country. You're not running growth from a desk. You're embedded in the communities we serve, acting as both a strategic thinker and a ground-level operator. You'll own growth outcomes for your assigned markets, working closely with Growth, Marketing, Product and Operations while serving as the internal voice of the user.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Travel across the USA 3 to 5 days every 2 to 3 weeks, establishing on-the-ground presence in new cities. Attend community events, drive brand awareness and new customer sign-ups. Build and expand LemFi's community network, network with local leaders and organizations, and arrange sponsorship for community events.

 - Develop and execute comprehensive user acquisition strategies tailored to Nigerian ex-pat communities in the US. Own growth targets for assigned markets and optimize campaigns using data and insights to improve acquisition KPIs.

 - Lead community-driven initiatives to build trust, awareness and advocacy for LemFi. Organize and participate in local events, meetups and forums. Build and maintain strong relationships with community leaders and grassroots partners. Act as brand ambassador for LemFi.

 - Identify, negotiate and manage partnerships with relevant organizations and businesses to accelerate user growth. Ensure partnerships deliver mutual value and measurable outcomes.

 - Conduct ongoing market research to understand user needs, behaviours and preferences. Provide actionable insights to Product and Marketing teams to refine messaging, features and positioning. Serve as the internal voice of the user, representing community perspectives in decision-making.




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - At least 2 years of experience in fast-paced environments with a background in growth, sales, marketing or similar.

 - Deep understanding of the Nigerian community in the United States. You're culturally fluent and comfortable immersing yourself in new cultural environments.

 - Proven ability to work against targets and deliver measurable results. You're motivated by ambitious goals and comfortable being measured against clear KPIs.

 - Strong data-driven mindset. You interpret data, track performance and adjust strategies accordingly.

 - Excellent interpersonal skills and the ability to build trust with diverse stakeholders. You're a persuasive communicator who can influence partners and leaders.

 - Entrepreneurial and tenacious. You take ownership, push through obstacles and find ways to win. Biased to action, you think critically and move quickly.

 - Self-motivated and effective working independently. You're willing to travel extensively and work occasional weekends to support market growth.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Prior experience launching or scaling ventures in new markets.

 - Background in fintech, payments or diaspora-focused products.

 - Established network within Nigerian diaspora communities.



WHY JOIN LEMFI?

Love s

source_jobs.title: Deputy General Counsel
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You'll be the second in command for LemFi's Legal Department, reporting directly to the Chief Legal Officer. This is high-impact, versatile leadership. You'll balance strategic advisory with hands-on execution across financing, M&A, corporate transactions, product launches, and global compliance. You'll be a key partner to the CLO, stepping in to lead the department in her absence and handling a broad portfolio of high-stakes legal matters. You're not managing a function from a distance. You're in the work, making the calls, and scaling the legal infrastructure as we expand globally.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Take ownership of legal structuring, negotiation and execution of complex credit and debt financing arrangements. Partner with Finance to secure and manage the capital that fuels our credit products and long-term growth.

 - Lead legal support for strategic transactions: M&A, fundraising and other high-value corporate matters. Manage due diligence, deal structuring and external counsel coordination to execute at speed and scale.

 - Oversee the legal administration of our Employee Stock Option Plans. Manage grants, vestings and exercises in compliance with local regulations across multiple jurisdictions.

 - In partnership with People, advise on global employment structures, cross-border employee arrangements and incentive schemes. Provide pragmatic, scalable legal input for our growing team.

 - Serve as the legal architect for new and evolving products. Collaborate with Product and Engineering to ensure offerings are compliant, risk-managed, and supported by clear customer documentation.

 - Partner with the CLO on corporate governance, capital markets and strategic legal projects. Lead and mentor the legal team and guide the use of external counsel.

 - Keep LemFi ahead of the curve in a complex international regulatory environment (FCA, FINTRAC, AUSTRAC) as we expand across Europe and new global markets.




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - 10+ years in a top-tier international law firm or in-house legal function, with a focus on banking, finance or corporate transactions.

 - Proven record advising on debt financings, credit facilities, security packages, plus experience with M&A or venture-backed equity.

 - Strong grasp of capital structures, investor documentation and multi-jurisdictional corporate governance.

 - Comfortable balancing legal risk with commercial reality. You know how to get to "yes" safely.

 - Excellent drafting, negotiation and stakeholder management skills. You communicate clearly to non-lawyers.

 - Confident advising leadership teams directly without large-firm infrastructure support.

 - Law degree from a reputable university and qualification to practise in the UK or Europe.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Familiarity with fintech or payments regulation.

 - In-house experience at a high-growth financial services or tech company.

 - Cross-border expansion or multi-market regulatory experience.



WHY JOIN LEMFI?

Love shouldn’t be expensive, yet those working hardest for their families often face predatory fees and banking exclusion. We're changing this.

At LemFi, you won’t be just a cog in a machine. Whether designing products, scaling operations, or telling our story, you’ll tackle complex challenges with real

source_jobs.title: Digital Growth Manager - Phillipines
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You'll drive LemFi's online user acquisition and engagement strategies across international markets, architecting a holistic digital ecosystem targeting the Southeast Asian diaspora globally. Your primary objective is to expand our reach and solidify brand presence among Filipino and broader Southeast Asian communities in the UK, USA, Europe, Canada and Australia. You won't just manage influencers. You'll scale influencer networks, forge B2B partnerships, cultivate engaged communities, and deliver measurable user growth and revenue. Working closely with Marketing, Product and Growth teams, you'll have the creative freedom and analytical backing to optimise campaigns that improve unit economics and connect the global Filipino community with home.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Scale influencer networks. Onboard, manage and grow an influencer roster to execute high-impact campaigns targeting the Southeast Asian diaspora globally.

 - Forge strategic partnerships. Develop B2B online growth partnerships and lead PR efforts with media channels and publications to amplify LemFi's global reach.

 - Build engaged communities. Cultivate active online communities that strengthen brand presence and drive organic conversions from sign-ups to transacting users.

 - Collaborate on content. Partner with designers and cross-functional teams to translate audience insights and market research into compelling, high-converting ad creatives. Work closely with the Paid Ads team to ensure a pipeline of conversion-driven content.

 - Own the metrics. Take direct accountability for key financial and growth indicators: new user sign-ups, conversion rates, CAC (cost of acquisition) and recurring revenue for the Philippines corridor.

 - Manage CRM functions. Track churn, manage the transactor lifecycle and collaborate with Finance, Legal and Compliance teams to ensure compliant, effective campaigns.

 - Act as cultural ambassador. Be the diaspora and cultural expert for the Philippines market during regional events and cross-functional planning.




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - Relevant experience in influencer management or digital growth roles, with hands-on expertise managing and optimising influencer or KOL campaigns across Google, Facebook, TikTok and YouTube.

 - Strong background in influencer marketing and community building, with B2B partnerships experience being a strong plus.

 - Deep understanding of ad content creation, A/B testing and campaign optimisation.

 - Highly analytical mindset. You interpret complex data to make smart strategic decisions that improve inventory efficiency and user retention, not just chase vanity metrics.

 - Excellent communication skills with the ability to interpret data and drive conversion rate optimisation from sign-up to transacting user.

 - Strategic and execution-focused. You combine big-picture thinking with hands-on approach, diving deep into campaign metrics to continuously optimise for ROI.

 - Relationship-driven. You naturally build strong connections with influencers, agencies and internal teams. Ideally you have an existing network within the Southeast Asian diaspora.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with CRM platforms and churn tracking.

 - Fintech, remittance or financial services background.

 - Adaptabilit

source_jobs.title: Product Analyst - Credit
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Credit is the heartbeat of our lending business. As a Product Analyst, you'll be the analytical engine driving the strategy that scales it. This means deep-dive investigations into portfolio performance, A/B testing to move the needle on product, and the kind of predictive work that lets senior leadership make high-stakes decisions fast, across the entire lending lifecycle.

You're embedded in the Credit function but sit at the technical bridge between Data Engineering, Risk, and Commercial. You won't just pull data. You'll read it, interpret it through a commercial lens, and tell the story behind the numbers. You need to be equally comfortable writing production SQL as you are presenting findings to the CFO.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Lead complex ad hoc investigations into portfolio performance and credit risk drivers, moving past surface-level metrics to uncover the hidden trends that matter.

 - Propose and set up product experiments on Statsig to improve Credit products based on analytical findings, then run the analysis to prove what worked.

 - Design, build and maintain robust data models in dbt, ensuring the credit data layer is scalable, version-controlled and optimized for speed.

 - Architect cohort models that drive unit economics, portfolio valuation and LTV projections. The kind of analysis that informs long-term strategy.

 - Proactively surface emerging risks and growth opportunities. Move from "what happened" to "what will happen" through rigorous analytical reasoning.

 - Deliver high-density deep-dives for strategic initiatives. Present technical findings with clarity and a clear "so-what" for senior stakeholders.

 - Partner with Decision Science and Product squads to make sure data-driven insights flow seamlessly into automated underwriting and product features.




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - Senior-level SQL and Python (Pandas, NumPy). You manipulate and analyse data with precision and efficiency.

 - Hands-on experience within a modern cloud data warehouse (Snowflake, BigQuery) and genuine mastery of dbt for data transformation and modelling.

 - Significant experience in a modern UK fintech or high-growth consumer lender. You understand the credit product lifecycle beyond traditional credit reporting.

 - A proven track record in advanced cohort analysis, portfolio valuations and financial modelling that have informed executive decisions.

 - Fluency in credit portfolio KPIs, risk metrics (PD, LGD, EAD) and the nuances of credit bureau data.

 - You translate complex technical analysis into actionable product or risk interventions that move the needle on business performance.

 - Fast-paced fintech mindset. You take full ownership of the analytical lifecycle and build scalable solutions, not quick fixes.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Hands-on experience with Statsig or similar experimentation platforms.

 - Familiarity with FCA and CONC regulatory frameworks.

 - Direct experience building or optimizing automated underwriting or credit decisioning systems.



WHY JOIN LEMFI?

Love shouldn’t be expensive, yet those working hardest for their families often face predatory fees and banking exclusion. We're changing this.

At LemFi, you won’t be just a cog in a machine. Whether designing products, scaling operations, or tel

source_jobs.title: Senior Accountant
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

LemFi's finance function is being built for scale while the company grows fast across the globe. As a Senior Accountant reporting to the Head of Finance, you'll help lay the foundations: scalable accounting processes, stronger financial controls, and reporting that lands accurately and on time across every entity.

This is a hands-on role for someone who likes building in a fast-moving fintech. You'll pair real technical accounting depth with a practical, problem-solving instinct, working across teams and with external partners to build a finance operation that is lean, reliable, and ready to scale.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Build scalable accounting and financial control processes that keep up with LemFi's growth, not ones you'll outgrow in a quarter.

 - Own the month-end close for your entities and deliver accurate management accounts on time, every time.

 - Prepare entity-level financial statements in line with applicable GAAP, and get every filing in on schedule.

 - Run point on audits. Work directly with external auditors, tax advisors and financial partners to keep them clean.

 - Strengthen internal controls, reporting systems and documentation so compliance and efficiency move together.

 - Partner with the data team to turn financial reporting into real visibility, using tools like Tableau and Looker.

 - Work across finance, data and business teams to line up accounting outputs with FP&A and what the business actually needs.




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - Relevant accounting experience, ideally in a fast-growth business: fintech, tech, or somewhere similarly dynamic.

 - ACA, ACCA, ICAEW, or an equivalent accounting qualification.

 - You've prepared statutory and non-statutory financial statements as well as monthly management accounts.

 - You've supported or led audits and worked directly with external auditors and financial partners.

 - A strong grip on accounting standards and financial controls, and the judgement to apply them in practice.

 - Confident working with financial data and building reporting processes that scale.

 - Proactive and organised. You find issues, dig into the root cause, and resolve them with minimal oversight.




NICE TO HAVE

 - NetSuite experience.

 - Familiarity with Tableau or Looker.



WHY JOIN LEMFI?

Love shouldn’t be expensive, yet those working hardest for their families often face predatory fees and banking exclusion. We're changing this.

At LemFi, you won’t be just a cog in a machine. Whether designing products, scaling operations, or telling our story, you’ll tackle complex challenges with real, immediate impact. Your work goes beyond metrics - it puts money back in families’ pockets and offers access to the previously excluded. Join us to make a meaningful difference, where high performance is a lifeline for millions.

You can connect with us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/lemfi/ and Instagram https://www.instagram.com/uselemfi/ and if you haven't already, download the app on the App Store https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/lemfi/id1533066809 or Google Play https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?hl=en&id=com.lemonadeFinance.android.

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager - Credit
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Credit is LemFi's fastest-growing pillar. As a Senior Product Manager, you'll own the roadmap that scales it. That means setting sharp priorities, turning portfolio insight into shippable outcomes, and making the commercial trade-offs between growth, risk, customer experience, and unit economics. You'll work shoulder-to-shoulder with engineering, risk, decision science, and commercial teams. You need to think like a portfolio manager. You'll live in the data yourself, not wait for someone to tell you what it says.

This is hands-on work for someone who understands that running a credit product means managing a live portfolio with real money at stake, not just shipping features.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Set sharp priorities across your product area, balancing growth, risk, customer experience and unit economics. Make the trade-offs that move the needle.

 - Get into the detail of portfolio performance. Convert that insight into product changes that move the metrics that matter: conversion, loss rates, activation, retention.

 - Use AI deliberately to remove friction from internal workflows, speed up discovery and prototyping, and unlock product experiences that wouldn't otherwise be economic to build.

 - Work shoulder-to-shoulder with engineering on requirements, edge cases, QA and launch. Not ceremonies, real partnership.

 - Partner with the Credit Product Lead to deliver end-to-end across acquisition, underwriting, servicing and collections.

 - Keep Credit Risk, Decision Science, Operations, Compliance and Commercial aligned and bought in. Protect the team's focus while managing the stakeholders.

 - Track KPIs across acquisition, penetration, retention, loss and unit economics. Communicate results and trade-offs clearly to leadership.




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - 5+ years of product management in consumer credit, lending or fintech, with hands-on exposure to managing a live lending portfolio.

 - Prior experience in credit risk, marketing analytics, finance or data science. You've spent meaningful time in an analytical function, not just consumed dashboards.

 - Strong commercial instinct. Fluent in unit economics, loss rates, conversion funnels and vintage performance.

 - SQL fluency or equivalent. You don't wait on an analyst to form a point of view.

 - Demonstrable track record using AI tools to drive efficiency, quality and delivery speed. Specific examples, not just buzzwords.

 - You've owned a roadmap and shipped outcomes that moved meaningful business metrics.

 - Strong cross-functional stakeholder management across risk, engineering, operations and commercial.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Familiarity with credit decisioning, scorecards or decision engines.

 - Experience scaling a credit product from early stage through rapid growth.



WHY JOIN LEMFI?

Love shouldn’t be expensive, yet those working hardest for their families often face predatory fees and banking exclusion. We're changing this.

At LemFi, you won’t be just a cog in a machine. Whether designing products, scaling operations, or telling our story, you’ll tackle complex challenges with real, immediate impact. Your work goes beyond metrics - it puts money back in families’ pockets and offers access to the previously excluded. Join us to make a meaningful diffe

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Millions of people trust LemFi with their money. That trust is won or lost at the interface. As a Senior Product Designer, you'll own the look, feel and polish of core product experiences: typography, layout, colour, motion, micro-interaction, and the design system that holds it all together. You craft the details others miss and set the visual bar across the app.

You don't need fintech experience. You need a portfolio that proves you ship beautiful, coherent consumer products at scale. Many great designers started in visual, brand or digital design and grew into product. If that's your path, this role plays to your strengths. You're hands-on, fast, and happiest making things look and feel exceptional.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Own end-to-end design for core product flows, from concept through to shipped UI.

 - Set and raise the visual bar across the LemFi app. Craft the details others miss.

 - Build and evolve the design system, including components, documentation and patterns that scale across squads and markets.

 - Bring brand and product together so the app feels unmistakably LemFi at every touchpoint.

 - Partner closely with product managers and engineers to ship pixel-accurate work fast.

 - Use AI tools as a native part of your workflow, from ideation and art direction to production.




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - 7+ years in digital design, with recent years in product design for consumer-facing apps or platforms.

 - A portfolio of shipped work showing exceptional UI craft and considered visual judgement.

 - Hands-on design systems experience: building component libraries, documentation and governance.

 - Expert-level Figma, plus fluency with AI-assisted design tools.

 - Experience working with engineers on implementation and handover.

 - Comfortable across the full product process (discovery, testing, iteration) but happiest making things look and feel exceptional.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Brand design or art direction experience.

 - Motion design expertise.

 - Multi-market or multilingual product experience.



WHY JOIN LEMFI?

Love shouldn’t be expensive, yet those working hardest for their families often face predatory fees and banking exclusion. We're changing this.

At LemFi, you won’t be just a cog in a machine. Whether designing products, scaling operations, or telling our story, you’ll tackle complex challenges with real, immediate impact. Your work goes beyond metrics - it puts money back in families’ pockets and offers access to the previously excluded. Join us to make a meaningful difference, where high performance is a lifeline for millions.

You can connect with us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/lemfi/ and Instagram https://www.instagram.com/uselemfi/ and if you haven't already, download the app on the App Store https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/lemfi/id1533066809 or Google Play https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?hl=en&id=com.lemonadeFinance.android.

source_jobs.title: Social Media Manager
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You'll shape how LemFi shows up online to UK diaspora communities. This is creative, video-first work where your job is to make LemFi feel relevant, authentic, and worth talking about across Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, YouTube and X. You'll turn cultural moments, community conversations and real stories into content that moves people. This role asks something specific of you: you need to understand these communities not just as an audience, but as communities you're genuinely part of or deeply connected to. You'll spot trends before they go mainstream, move fast, and build spaces where people feel seen and heard.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Own and manage LemFi's social channels across Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, YouTube and X. Transform X into a genuine engagement channel. Shape how we show up across every platform.

 - Develop a video-first content calendar that reflects the real lives and interests of UK diaspora communities. Write sharp captions and copy that feels native to each platform.

 - Spot cultural trends and conversations before they go mainstream. Move quickly when they do. Build and nurture online communities where audiences feel seen, heard and connected.

 - Identify, engage and manage relationships with creators, influencers and community partners who speak authentically to our audience. Build genuine relationships that matter.

 - Direct and collaborate with videographers, photographers and content creators to bring ideas to life. Monitor performance metrics and use insights to continuously improve strategy.

 - Help shape LemFi's brand voice and social strategy. Approach all content as storytelling, not marketing.




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - 3 to 5+ years of social media experience, ideally at a brand with a multicultural or diaspora-facing audience.

 - Strong content creation skills, especially video, with a portfolio that demonstrates cultural relevance and creative instinct.

 - Deep understanding of UK diaspora communities and how they engage with brands on social media. You're connected to these communities, not observing from outside.

 - Hands-on experience across Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, YouTube and X.

 - Experience managing influencer and content creator relationships.

 - Strong written communication and community management skills.

 - Proactive mindset. You spot opportunities and act on them without waiting to be told.

 - Aesthetic sense. You know what looks good and why it works on each platform.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience working with multicultural brands or community organisations.

 - Video editing skills: CapCut, Canva, Adobe Creative Suite or similar.

source_jobs.title: US Deputy Compliance Officer
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

LemFi is building the financial infrastructure for the world's immigrant communities. We operate across roughly 21 countries, hold money transmission licences across the United States, and work within a sponsor bank structure. Compliance here is a strategic function, not a checkbox, and the US programme is one of the most complex and consequential parts of the business.
This role sits at the centre of the US compliance operation, reporting to the US Chief Compliance Officer and covering both BSA/AML and consumer compliance. You will be the operational lead on day-to-day compliance running, the first escalation point for the business, and the person who gives the CCO the confidence to focus on the most strategic and complex matters. For the right person, this role grows significantly as LemFi scales.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Run the day-to-day BSA/AML operation: SAR quality, FinCEN regulatory reporting, and transaction monitoring, escalating material issues and deputising for the CCO when they are out.

 - Own the consumer compliance programme operationally: UDAAP, Reg E, CAN-SPAM, and TCPA reviews of marketing and advertising materials, plus disclosure maintenance and complaints intake through to resolution.

 - Lead the consumer complaints programme end-to-end, spotting systemic issues and proposing root-cause fixes rather than just closing tickets.

 - Support multi-state MTL compliance, including licence renewals, regulatory notifications, and examination preparation across all active LemFi US states.

 - Manage sponsor bank compliance commitments, keeping the status documented, open items tracked, and the CCO informed before things become surprises.

 - Contribute to US compliance sections of board and executive management reports, and support the group-wide compliance risk assessment.

 - Use AI tools to accelerate policy drafting, evidence pack preparation, and regulatory research, freeing your own capacity for the judgement calls that actually need you.


YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - 6 or more years of compliance experience in US payments, money transmission, fintech, or financial services, with genuine hands-on depth in both BSA/AML and consumer compliance.

 - Solid working knowledge of BSA/AML requirements, FinCEN regulations, and OFAC, including direct experience with SAR and CTR processes.

 - Experience contributing to a consumer compliance programme covering UDAAP, Reg E, and complaints management, not just advising on it.

 - Familiarity with multi-state MTL compliance and the regulatory expectations of state money transmission regulators.

 - Experience working within or alongside a sponsor bank or BaaS compliance structure, and an understanding of how those relationships work under pressure.

 - Strong written and verbal communication: able to produce clear compliance guidance, policy documents, and regulatory correspondence that the business and regulators can both rely on.

 - Comfortable operating with limited day-to-day supervision, managing competing priorities across two compliance workstreams, and making sound judgement calls independently.


NICE TO HAVE

 - CAMS certification (or near completion); CRCM is a further plus.

 - Prior involvement in regulatory examinations or supervisory matters on BSA/AML or con

source_jobs.title: Head of Performance Marketing
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

LemFi is building the financial platform for immigrants: borderless accounts, remittances, savings, credit, and eSIMs across ~21 countries. Our users are real people building lives across borders, and performance marketing is the engine that finds them first. Reporting to the CMO, the Head of Performance Marketing owns our global paid acquisition strategy across every corridor we run, from UK-to-Nigeria to Canada-to-India, and is accountable for translating media spend into transacting, retained users.

This role balances high-level strategic ownership with hands-on execution. You will lead, mentor, and advocate for an experienced, highly autonomous international team running our international corridors—while remaining hands-on to directly manage global campaigns on core channels like Google, ASA, and programmatic platforms.

This role requires taking full strategic authority back from top-down leadership, driving company-level visibility for a team that delivers over 45% of user acquisition, and building our critical data and attribution infrastructure from the ground up. If you want to manage spreadsheets, this isn't for you. If you want to champion an exceptional team and build the acquisition machine for a category-defining fintech, keep reading.




WHAT YOU’LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Own the global paid acquisition strategy, making the final call on outcomes while staying hands-on to directly run and optimise global campaigns across Google, Apple Search Ads (ASA), and programmatic networks.

 - Empower and guide our highly autonomous regional experts running the African and Asian corridors, collaborating with them on their pre-defined budgets, channel allocations, and local executions.

 - Rebuild our measurement and attribution capabilities from scratch, moving the organisation away from last-click standards to track deep-funnel performance, revenue impact, LTV, and payback periods.

 - Serve as the primary cross-functional advocate for the performance marketing team, securing crucial resources like Data Engineering, Data Science, and third-party tools from senior leadership.

 - Partner with the creative and marketing teams to eliminate bandwidth bottlenecks, accelerating the creative pipeline to scale up ad volume and variety.

 - Review, resource, and execute the team's drafted AI roadmap, defining clear expectations for AI-powered creative production and workflow automation.

 - Integrate paid acquisition efforts into the broader growth ecosystem, aligning paid media with organic, social, and influencer channels to drive optimal business outcomes.

 - Own the install-to-transfer funnel, working tightly with Product, Data, and Compliance to ensure paid traffic doesn't just download the app but clears KYC and completes a first funded transfer.




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - 8+ years in digital, paid performance, or growth marketing; fintech, mobile consumer apps, remittance, or high-growth consumer SaaS strongly preferred.

 - Proven track record running large monthly paid media budgets across Google, ASA, Meta, and TikTok with a consistent record of hitting CPA and payback targets at scale.

 - Strong leadership and team ownership experience, with proven success managing and mentoring international, cross-cultural teams.

 - Excellent in

source_jobs.title: Head of Francophone Africa
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

LemFi moves money for Francophone diaspora communities across Sub-Saharan and North Africa — corridors that are high-stakes, fast-moving, and deeply underserved. As Head of Francophone Region, you will own the full commercial outcome for these markets: the P&L, the team, the partnerships, and the product launches that unlock the next phase of growth. You will report to the Head of Africa, lead a growth team distributed across Europe, and carry direct accountability for take-rate, revenue, and margin targets across two distinct sub-regions.


This is not a strategy-only role. You will set the regional direction and then get into the detail — pricing corridors, signing payout partners, launching cash pick-up in North Africa, deepening penetration in established Sub-Saharan markets. You will be the internal voice of the Francophone region, shaping product roadmaps and go-to-market decisions, and the external face of LemFi to partners and regulators. If you want ownership, complexity, and a seat at a table that genuinely matters, this is it.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD & OWN

 - Own the Francophone region P&L end-to-end — revenue, take rate, margin — and make the calls needed to hit targets, including pricing, resource allocation, and partner decisions.

 - Lead and scale the regional growth team across Europe: set goals, build a high-performance culture, hire great people, and cut loose anything holding the region back.

 - Launch new products across North Africa — starting with cash pick-up — from market entry through to real adoption, working hand-in-hand with Product and Operations.

 - Scale existing products across Sub-Saharan Francophone corridors: deepen penetration, improve retention, and turn insight into commercial action.

 - Set and oversee the partnership strategy — payout partners, banks, agent networks, fintechs — ensuring every relationship delivers network coverage, commercial value, and compliance.

 - Drive user acquisition and activation across online and offline channels, ensuring growth and marketing strategies land with Francophone diaspora and in-market audiences.

 - Use data and AI tools to model performance, sharpen forecasts, and bring the region's voice clearly into product, pricing, and go-to-market decisions across the wider business.




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - Professional fluency in French and English — written and spoken; you can run a board meeting in either language.

 - Proven P&L ownership in a fintech, payments, or remittance business — you have hit take-rate, margin, and revenue targets and can show the numbers.

 - Track record leading and scaling commercial or growth teams across multiple countries, ideally in a high-growth, distributed environment.

 - Deep knowledge of Francophone African markets — Sub-Saharan and North Africa — including the regulatory, cultural, and competitive dynamics that actually matter.

 - Demonstrated success building commercial partnerships, including payout networks, cash pick-up infrastructure, or comparable fintech distribution channels.

 - Strong analytical instincts — you set strategy from data, not instinct, and you can translate complex performance signals into decisive action.

 - Willingness and ability to travel across the region and Europe regularly as the business

source_jobs.title: Growth Launcher, Europe > Nigeria
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

LemFi is expanding fast across Europe, and Nigerian diaspora communities are at the heart of that growth. We're building culturally rooted, community-first growth strategies — not generic ad campaigns — and we need someone who can own that work end-to-end in one of our key European markets: Germany, Spain, Italy, or Ireland.


This is a deeply entrepreneurial role. You'll be the in-market face of LemFi — running user acquisition, forging partnerships, showing up at community events, and feeding real on-the-ground insight back into the product and marketing teams. If you thrive when you're building from scratch, moving fast, and making things happen with limited playbook, this is your role.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD & OWN

 - Design and execute user acquisition strategies for your assigned European market — online and offline, measurable and iterated relentlessly.

 - Own community-led growth: plan and run events, meetups, and cultural activations that build genuine trust and accelerate word-of-mouth.

 - Identify, pitch, and land partnerships with diaspora businesses, community organisations, and local influencers — then manage them for real impact.

 - Be the in-market voice of the user: conduct regular market research, surface cultural insights, and brief the product and marketing teams on what actually matters to your community.

 - Track your acquisition funnel and partnership performance with data — use AI tools to synthesise insights faster, spot trends, and sharpen your next move.

 - Represent LemFi with clarity and conviction: you're not just a brand ambassador, you're the reason people in your city trust us with their money.

 - Travel across your market and occasionally across Europe for events, activations, and community moments — flexibility is part of the brief.




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - 2–4 years in community growth, field marketing, partnerships, or go-to-market roles — fintech, diaspora, or multicultural environments strongly preferred.

 - Deep, lived familiarity with Nigerian diaspora communities in Europe — you understand the culture, the trust dynamics, and the money habits.

 - Proven track record of hitting acquisition or growth targets, not just running activities.

 - Strong communicator who can influence community leaders, local partners, and internal stakeholders equally well.

 - Comfortable working independently and remotely — you don't need a manager to tell you what to do next.

 - Analytically sharp: you're at ease with dashboards, acquisition metrics, and iterating based on what the data says.

 - Fluency in English required; working proficiency in the local language of your market (German, Spanish, Italian) is a strong plus.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in remittances, multi-currency accounts, or other cross-border financial products.

 - Existing relationships within Nigerian diaspora networks in your target market.

 - Familiarity with AI tools for content creation, research, or campaign analysis.



WHY JOIN LEMFI?

LemFi is the financial engine for people the world wasn't built to serve — and nowhere is that more true than for the Ghanaian diaspora in the UK. This role exists to own that community: getting LemFi in front of the right people, at the right moments, through channels that actually move them. You'll be on the ground in cities across the UK one week and stress-testing acquisition data the next.


You'll sit inside the Growth team but operate like a founder for your market — embedded in communities, trusted by local leaders, and accountable for hard numbers. What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.

LemFi is building the financial home for the African diaspora — and Canada is one of our most important frontiers. The Nigerian, Ghanaian, East African, and Francophone African communities across Canada are large, fast-growing, and deeply underserved by the banks they've been handed. This role exists to change that.


As Growth Manager – Canada (African Corridors), you will own user acquisition and community presence across the country. You'll be on the ground at events in Toronto one week and Vancouver the next, negotiating partnerships with community organisations, feeding product teams real user insight, and relentlessly closing the gap between LemFi and the people we're built to serve. This is a high-ownership, high-visibility role for someone who thrives without a playbook.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD & OWN

 - Drive user acquisition across the Nigerian, Ghanaian, East African, and Francophone African corridors in Canada — owning your targets end to end, online and offline.

 - Strong analytical skills — you're comfortable in dashboards, can interrogate campaign data, and adjust strategy based on what the numbers say.

 - Excellent communicator — you can command a room at a community event and write a tight partnership proposal the same day.

 - Comfortable with frequent travel across Canada, including occasional weekends.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in fintech, remittances, or financial services — especially products serving immigrant or diaspora communities.

 - French language proficiency to support Francophone African corridor engagement.

 - Familiarity with AI tools to accelerate content creation, market research, and performance analysis.

source_jobs.title: Performance Marketing Executive - French Speaking

LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.

LemFi is the financial home for people the world wasn't built to serve — and French-speaking Africa is one of our most important growth frontiers. This role sits at the heart of that mission: you'll own paid acquisition across the corridors that matter, driving real user growth across Meta, TikTok, Google App Campaigns, Apple Search Ads, and beyond.

You'll operate inside the core Performance Marketing team, reporting to the Senior Performance Marketing Manager, working cross-functionally with Creative, Product, Data, and CRM. This is not a campaign-manager-by-numbers role. You'll own the full growth loop — from audience strategy and channel mix to creative briefing, measurement, and structured experimentation. If you love moving fast, hate wasted spend, and know how to turn performance data into culturally resonant content that actually converts, this is your role.

 


WHAT YOU'LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Highly self-directed: you identify the opportunity, build the plan, and execute — without waiting to be told.

 


NICE TO HAVE

source_jobs.title: Growth Operations Manager, China
source_jobs.description:
LemFi (Series B) is building the go-to financial app for the Global South.


Moving to a new country shouldn’t mean starting from zero. That's why our team of 400+ spanning 20+ countries is building a financial ecosystem that helps immigrants stay connected to home, build stability, and create wealth regardless of where they are from or where they live.


What began as fast, affordable remittances is now evolving into a complete platform for multi-currency accounts, payments, credit, and long-term financial growth.


With millions of users across the globe, we process over $1B in monthly transactions to 30+ countries, proving that borders shouldn't limit financial opportunity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a bilingual Growth Operations Manager to own LemFi's presence across Chinese digital platforms and drive user growth within the Chinese diaspora globally. This is a creative, fast-paced role sitting at the intersection of content, social media, and operations. You'll shape content strategy, manage Chinese social platforms, support product localisation, run CRM campaigns, and support payment operations. Above all, you'll be the voice of LemFi for Chinese communities overseas, turning real cultural insight into content that resonates and drives growth.


You'll report into the Growth team and work cross-functionally with Product, Operations and CRM to execute against a clear growth agenda. You'll work independently, own your impact end-to-end, and use data plus cultural fluency to iterate fast.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD AND OWN

 - Own and manage LemFi's Chinese social media channels (Rednote, Weibo, WeChat, Instagram, Facebook) with a monthly content calendar that reflects the real lives and financial needs of Chinese diaspora communities

 - Develop and execute culturally resonant content strategy, spot platform trends and cultural moments, and move fast to capitalize on them

 - Build and nurture online communities where LemFi's Chinese audience feels seen and connected, using data to continuously sharpen strategy and measure impact

 - Translate and localise product content across mobile, web and blog via our CMS, maintaining brand voice and cultural accuracy while optimizing for SEO

 - Plan, execute and optimise customer engagement campaigns via email and push notifications to support product launches and retention, managing segmentation, testing and performance tracking

 - Support daily funding operations and liquidity monitoring across payment channels, coordinating fund allocation and FX activities to ensure smooth transaction flow

 - Leverage AI tools to ideate, create and iterate content faster, freeing you to focus on strategy and community building




YOUR EXPERIENCE

 - Native or near-native Mandarin Chinese with C1+ business English

 - 2-3 years' hands-on experience in social media, content creation, marketing or growth, ideally in fintech, tech or consumer internet

 - Proven fluency managing Chinese digital platforms (Rednote, Weibo, WeChat) with strong cultural and platform-specific judgment

 - Confident using AI tools for content ideation, copywriting and localisation to work faster and at scale

 - Highly organised and able to manage multiple workstreams independently; you move fast and own your outcomes




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience at a fintech or cross-border payments company

 - Hands-on familiarity with CRM tools and campaign management

 - Background working with or marketing to diaspora or immigrant communities



WHY JOIN LEMFI?

Love shouldn’t be expensive, yet those working hardest for their families often face predatory fees and banking exclusion. We're changing this.

At LemFi, you won’t be just a cog in a machine. Whether designing products, scaling operations, or telling our story, you’ll tackle complex challenges with real, immediate impact. Your work goes beyond metrics - it puts money back in families’ pockets and offers access to the previously excluded. Join us to make a meanin

notes: LemFi is a financial app for the Global South, with a strong focus on growth and operations roles.
